title: Array Basics Quiz
description: Test your understanding of arrays — indexing, traversal, and common operations.

questions.q: What is the time complexity of accessing an element by index in an array?
Which of the following is NOT a valid operation on a static array?
What does arr.length return in JavaScript for arr = [10, 20, 30]?
Which method removes the last element of an array in JavaScript?
What is the output of [1,2,3].indexOf(2)?

title: Time & Space Complexity Quiz
description: Sharpen your Big-O intuition with 5 algorithmic complexity questions.

questions.q: What is the time complexity of Binary Search?
Which sorting algorithm has the best average-case time complexity?
What is the space complexity of a recursive Fibonacci function (no memoization)?
For a hash map lookup, the average-case time complexity is:
Which of these has O(n²) worst-case time complexity?

title: Linked List Concepts
description: 5 questions covering singly vs doubly linked lists, nodes, and traversal.

questions.q: In a singly linked list, each node contains:
What is the time complexity of inserting at the head of a linked list?
Which of these structures allows O(1) access by index?
To detect a cycle in a linked list, which algorithm is commonly used?
In a doubly linked list, the head node's 'prev' pointer points to:

title: Stack & Queue Quiz
description: 5 quick questions on stack and queue data structures and their operations.

questions.q: Which data structure follows the LIFO principle?
What does 'push' do in a stack?
A Queue follows which principle?
Which operation removes from the front of a queue?
A stack can be implemented using:

title: OOP Principles Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, and abstraction.

questions.q: Which OOP concept allows a class to inherit properties from another class?
Method overloading is an example of:
Which access modifier makes a member accessible only within the same class?
An abstract class in Java:
options: Can have both abstract and concrete methods

questions.q: Which SOLID principle states a class should have only one reason to change?

title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers and a target, return indices of two numbers that add up to the target.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Determine if an input string of brackets is valid using a stack-based approach.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Use the sliding window technique to find the longest substring with unique characters.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge two sorted linked lists and return it as a new sorted list.

title: Maximum Subarray (Kadane's)
description: Apply Kadane's Algorithm to find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum.

title: Read About Database Indexing
description: Read at least 2 articles on how B-Tree indexes speed up SQL queries. Take notes.

title: Watch: System Design — URL Shortener
description: Watch a system design video on building a URL shortener (TinyURL). Note the key components.

title: Revise Git Branching Strategy
description: Review Git Flow and Trunk-Based development. Understand when to use feature branches.
